title: citrusy lazy-sunday grønnsakstove
description: a hearty, slow-cooked norwegian-inspired dish made with tender root vegetables, citrus, and a hint of vinegar, perfect for a relaxing sunday afternoon.
instructions: season the lamb shoulder with salt, pepper, and thyme.
heat the olive oil in a large dutch oven over medium-high heat. sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
add the diced onions to the pot and cook until they begin to soften, about 3-4 minutes. add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
add the sliced carrots, parsnips, and turnips to the pot, stirring to combine with the onions and garlic. cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
add the white wine vinegar, apple cider vinegar, lemon juice, and water to the pot, stirring to combine. bring the mixture to a simmer.
return the lamb shoulder to the pot, cover with a lid, and transfer to the preheated oven. cook for 2-3 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. serve the lamb and vegetables hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
season with salt and pepper to taste.
serve with additional lemon wedges on the side, if desired.
reheat the remaining liquid from the pot and serve as a sauce over the lamb and vegetables, if desired.
